title: Badgers ready for Limpopo Impala
description: GEORGE NEWS - The Six Gun Grill Garden Route...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - The Six Gun Grill Garden Route Badgers will take on the Limpopo Impalas in the Cricket South Africa Division 2 Four-day Competition this weekend.

Although the Badgers lost their previous fixture, against the Tuskers, they are still contenders in this competition, but a victory against the Impalas will be non-negotiable.


> The Badgers will have home ground advantage at the Recreation Ground.


The Badgers are currently third on the log with 28.16 points after two matches.

The Tuskers are leading at 60.72 points after three matches, followed by the Northern Cape Heat at 55.44 points after three matches. The Limpopo Impalas are in fifth place at 23.54 points after winning the one match they have played so far.

Keenan Vieira, who impressed with the bat for the Colts team, will be unavailable for selection due to a fractured finger, but Ruan Terblanche who has been sidelined for the season thus far is fit and will be considered for selection.


> Coach Gurshwin Rabie has also added the 18-year-old spin bowler, Enathi Kitshini, to the squad and it is very likely that he will make his first class debut for the Badgers.


‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
